beats.activity: Wake up, coffee, and quick morning routine at home.
beats.reasoning: Lily wakes early as is her habit; low energy yesterday (45/100) means she'll keep this beat short and skip the dawn trail run today to recharge.

beats.activity: Morning run around Lake Union with film camera.
beats.reasoning: Friday warm weather (77F) and breezy conditions are perfect for an outdoor run; Lily never misses this ritual and will want to capture the good light with her camera before the day gets busy.

beats.activity: Drive from Lake Union to Harborview Medical Center downtown.
beats.reasoning: Commute from Capitol Hill to downtown; Lily drives to work on ER shifts, budgeting 35 min for Friday morning traffic crossing the city.

beats.activity: ER shift: triage, intake, patient care.
beats.reasoning: Friday morning and early afternoon ER shift; Lily works a typical 8:20am–1pm block before her lunch break.

beats.activity: Lunch break: grab a salad and wander Pike Place Market.
beats.reasoning: Lily's Friday ritual; warm weather means she'll linger longer at Pike Place, watching buskers and enjoying the outdoor atmosphere before heading back to the hospital.

beats.activity: Afternoon/evening ER shift: continued patient care.
beats.reasoning: Extended afternoon shift until early evening; Lily clocks out before the Pride street fair chaos builds on Broadway and Pike.

beats.activity: Drive home to Leschi to drop off work gear and freshen up.
beats.reasoning: Lily heads home after work; downtown ER commute back to Leschi budgets 40 min given Friday evening traffic and Pride road closures affecting downtown/Capitol Hill routes.

beats.activity: Quick freshen-up and change at home before heading out.
beats.reasoning: Lily showers, changes into comfortable clothes for the evening; she's an optimist and enjoys community, and warm Friday Pride energy appeals to her after a long shift.

beats.activity: Drive to Capitol Hill Pride street fair.
beats.reasoning: Lily heads to the Pride kickoff on Broadway; warm weather and festive atmosphere align with her optimistic personality, though she'll avoid the peak crowd chaos by arriving early-ish.

beats.activity: Wander Capitol Hill Pride street fair, enjoy the community vibe, take photos with film camera.
beats.reasoning: Lily values community and close friends over big crowds, but Pride is a Seattle tradition she appreciates; warm breezy evening is ideal, and her film camera will capture good light. She'll leave before the late-night peak density.
Lily heads home after the Pride fair; Friday night late-commute traffic is lighter, 45 min budgeted for Capitol Hill back to Leschi via less-congested routes.

beats.activity: Wind down at home reviewing film from the day, organize vinyl collection, then sleep.
beats.reasoning: Lily unwinds with her film and vinyl—her favorite ritual after a social outing and long shift. She'll review the day's camera shots and organize records before bed.
Lily gets a full 7.25 hours of sleep, ending by 6:30am Saturday morning; the overnight beat spans across midnight as required.

diary:
Started the day like I always do—coffee and the lake. My run around Union felt good, camera in hand, the light just right. Work was steady at Harborview; a Friday morning in the ER is its own kind of rhythm. Grabbed a salad at Pike Place during lunch and just wandered for a bit, needing that quiet.

After shift I rushed home to change, still buzzing from the morning's adrenaline. But when I got to Capitol Hill for Pride, something shifted. The energy there was different—so much joy, so much color and music. I walked around with my film camera, capturing people, moments, the feeling of it all. It reminded me why I do what I do. Headed home as the sun was setting, spent the evening reviewing my photos and organizing my vinyl. Felt peaceful, like the day held something real.
thought_process: I think I needed today to feel balanced. The ER drains you—the urgency, the heaviness of it—so I built in those moments for myself: the run with my camera, the market wandering. When Pride came around, I almost talked myself out of going, tired as I was. But I pushed through because part of me knew I needed to be around that kind of community energy. The contrast between the clinical morning and the celebratory evening actually grounded me. It's easy to forget why we show up for people when you're in the thick of it. The warmth and the crowds reminded me that there's more to living than just surviving shifts.
world_event_prompt: Warm, breezy Friday, high of 77F. Seattle Pride kickoff: Capitol Hill street fair and road closures along Broadway and Pike from 5pm. Festive, busy evening downtown.
